title: What Do Metal Roofing Specialists Do?
content:
You may be wondering, what do metal roofing specialists actually do? Well, you've come to the right place; this article will inform you of the services that metal roofing specialist offer.

Metal roofing services usually includes installation, repair, replacement, and maintenance. Here at Sandhurst Roofing we offer comprehensive services to people across Melbourne.

Metal Roofing Services

  • Metal roofing installation: This service involves the installation of a new metal roof system, either for new construction or as a replacement for an existing roof.
  • Metal roof repair: Metal roofing repair services include fixing leaks, replacing damaged panels, and addressing issues with flashing, among other things.
  • Metal roof replacement: This service involves removing an old or damaged metal roof and replacing it with a new one.
  • Metal roof restoration: Metal roof restoration services can include cleaning, painting, and applying a protective coating to extend the life of the roof and improve its appearance.
  • Metal roof maintenance: Regular maintenance services can include inspections, cleaning, and repairing small issues before they turn into major problems.

It's important to choose a professional roofing company with experience in metal roofing and a good reputation for quality work. They can help you determine the best metal roofing options for your needs and provide reliable, efficient services to keep your roof in top condition.

Choosing A Metal Roofing Specialist

When choosing a metal roof specialist in Melbourne, it's important to do your research and choose a reputable company with experience in metal roofing installations. Look for companies with positive reviews, experience in the industry, and a commitment to quality workmanship. Well, you've come to the right place! Sandhurst Roofing are one of Melbourne's finest, most experienced Metal Roofing Specialists.

With over 30 years of experience, Sandhurst Roofing are the team for you. Over the years we’ve grown to become one of Melbourne’s leading roofing companies. Competitive pricing and honest advice are our hallmarks. As a result, we can help you solve any roofing issue that you may have.

Advantages & Disadvantages Of A Metal Roof

Advantages

  • Durability: Metal roofs are extremely durable and can last up to 50 years or more, depending on the type of metal used and the installation process.
  • Low maintenance: Metal roofs require very little maintenance compared to other types of roofing materials. They do not rot, crack, or warp and are resistant to pests.
  • Energy efficient: Metal roofs can reflect heat from the sun, which can help reduce cooling costs during the summer months.
  • Lightweight: Metal roofs are relatively lightweight compared to other roofing materials, which can make installation easier and less costly.
  • Fire-resistant: Metal roofs are resistant to fire, which can help protect your home in the event of a wildfire.

Disadvantages

  • Cost: Metal roofs can be more expensive than other types of roofing materials, such as asphalt shingles.
  • Noise: Rain or hail hitting a metal roof can be quite loud, which may be a concern for some homeowners.
  • Expansion and contraction: Metal roofs can expand and contract with changes in temperature, which can cause them to make noise or create gaps over time.
  • Denting: Metal roofs can be more prone to denting than other types of roofing materials, which can be a concern if you live in an area with frequent hailstorms.
  • Installation: Proper installation of a metal roof requires a high level of skill and experience, which can make it more expensive than other types of roofing materials.

Metal roofs can last for a very long time, often more than 50 years, depending on several factors such as the type of metal, quality of installation, and maintenance. Here are some estimates for the lifespan of various types of metal roofing materials:

  • Galvanized steel: 15-30 years
  • Aluminum: 30-50 years
  • Copper: 50+ years
  • Zinc: 50+ years

The lifespan of a metal roof can also be affected by environmental factors, such as extreme weather conditions and exposure to saltwater or pollutants. Regular maintenance and inspections can help identify and address any issues early, which can extend the lifespan of the roof.

The cost of painting versus replacing a metal roof depends on several factors, such as the condition of the roof, the size of the roof, the cost of materials and labor in your area, and the type of paint or roofing material you choose.

In general, painting a metal roof is typically less expensive than replacing it. Painting a metal roof can help extend its lifespan, prevent rust and corrosion, and improve its overall appearance. However, if your metal roof is severely damaged or has reached the end of its lifespan, replacing it may be a better long-term solution.

It's best to get a professional opinion on whether your metal roof needs to be painted or replaced, and to get a quote for both options so you can make an informed decision based on your budget and the condition of your roof.

Metal roofs do not necessarily need to be painted, but painting can help protect the roof from rust and corrosion, and can also improve its aesthetic appearance. For more information about painting metal roofs, click here.
